---
title: "SIM - Deaths (individual-level)"
author: "Abirami Somasundaram"
date: "2026-06-29"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)
library(arrow)
library(purrr)
library(scales)
library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)
library(ggh4x)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r read-data}
df <- read_parquet("/Users/abiramisomasundaram/Desktop/Brazil pesticides/Data/Dados Version 2/Dados/resultados/SIM/sim_iexo_sp_2014_2024.parquet") |> 

  as_tibble()

glimpse(df)

```
2. Define variables
```{r define-variables}
date_col <- "DTOBITO"
age_col <- "IDADEanos"

categorical_vars <- c(
  "CAUSABAS",
  "CAUSABAS_O",
  "CODMUNRES",
  "CODMUNOCOR",
  "SEXO",
  "RACACOR",
  "ESC2010",
  "OCUP",
  "ACIDTRAB",
  "CIRCOBITO",
  "ASSISTMED"
)

all_analysis_vars <- c(date_col, age_col, categorical_vars)

# Check that all variables exist in the dataset
setdiff(all_analysis_vars, names(df))
```

3. Missingness function
```{r missingness-function}

is_missing_value <- function(x) {
  is.na(x) | 
    ifelse(is.character(x), str_squish(x) == "", FALSE)
}

missing_table <- function(data, vars) {
  map_dfr(vars, function(v) {
    x <- data[[v]]
    miss <- is_missing_value(x)
    
    tibble(
      variable = v,
      total_n = length(x),
      missing_n = sum(miss),
      missing_percent = round(100 * mean(miss), 2),
      non_missing_n = sum(!miss),
      non_missing_percent = round(100 * mean(!miss), 2)
    )
  })
}
```

4. Frequency table function

```{r frequency-function}

freq_table <- function(data, var, top_n = Inf) {
  
  total <- nrow(data)
  
  tab <- data |>
    transmute(category = as.character(.data[[var]])) |>
    mutate(
      category = str_squish(category),
      category = if_else(is.na(category) | category == "", "Missing", category)
    ) |>
    count(category, name = "n", sort = TRUE) |>
    mutate(percent = 100 * n / total)
  
  # keep only top N categories and combine the rest as "Other"
  if (is.finite(top_n)) {
    missing_row <- tab |> filter(category == "Missing")
    non_missing_rows <- tab |> filter(category != "Missing")
    
    top_rows <- non_missing_rows |> slice_head(n = top_n)
    
    other_rows <- anti_join(non_missing_rows, top_rows, by = "category") |>
      summarise(
        category = "Other",
        n = sum(n),
        percent = sum(percent),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) |>
      filter(n > 0)
    
    tab <- bind_rows(top_rows, other_rows, missing_row)
  }
  
  tab |>
    mutate(
      variable = var,
      percent = round(percent, 2)
    ) |>
    select(variable, category, n, percent)
}
```

4. Prepare date and age variables

```{r prepare-date-age}
df_analysis <- df |>
  mutate(
    death_date = ymd(.data[[date_col]]),
    death_year = year(death_date),
    death_calendar_quarter = paste0(year(death_date), "-Q", quarter(death_date)),
    age_years = parse_number(as.character(.data[[age_col]]))
  )
```

5. Missingness report
```{r missingness-report}
missing_results <- missing_table(df, all_analysis_vars)

kable(
  missing_results,
  caption = "Missingness by variable"
)
```

# 6. Frequency tables for categorical variables

```{r categorical-frequency-tables, results='asis'}
for (v in categorical_vars) {
  cat("\n\n## Frequency table:", v, "\n\n")
  
  tab <- freq_table(df_analysis, v)
  
  print(
    kable(
      tab,
      caption = paste("Frequency distribution for", v)
    )
  )
}
```


7. Frequency tables with top categories only

Some variables, such as municipality, ICD-10 code, and occupation, may have many categories.  
You may want to show only the top 20 categories and group the rest as `"Other"`.

```{r top-category-tables, results='asis'}
high_cardinality_vars <- c(
  "CAUSABAS",
  "CAUSABAS_O",
  "CODMUNRES",
  "CODMUNOCOR",
  "OCUP"
)

for (v in high_cardinality_vars) {
  cat("\n\n## Top 20 categories:", v, "\n\n")
  
  tab <- freq_table(df_analysis, v, top_n = 20)
  
  print(
    kable(
      tab,
      caption = paste("Top 20 frequency distribution for", v)
    )
  )
}
```


# 10. Date of death summaries

## By year

```{r death-year-frequency}
death_year_table <- freq_table(df_analysis, "death_year")

kable(
  death_year_table,
  caption = "Frequency distribution by year of death"
)
```


## By calendar quarter

```{r death-quarter-frequency}
death_quarter_table <- freq_table(df_analysis, "death_calendar_quarter")

kable(
  death_quarter_table,
  caption = "Frequency distribution by calendar quarter of death"
)
```
